title: Ensure unauthorized API calls are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure unauthorized API calls are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for unauthorized API calls.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring unauthorized API calls will help reduce the time it takes to detect malicious activity and can alert you to potential security incidents.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.errorCode ="*UnauthorizedOperation") || ($.errorCode ="AccessDenied*") && ($.sourceIPAddress!="delivery.logs.amazonaws.com") && ($.eventName!="HeadBucket") }", 6. Note the <unauthorized-api-calls-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <unauthorized-api-calls-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?MetricName == <unauthorized-api-calls-metric>]" 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for unauthorized API calls and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <unauthorized-api-calls-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=unauthorized_api_calls_metric,metricNamespace=CISBenchmark,metricValue=1 --filter-pattern "{ ($.errorCode ="*UnauthorizedOperation") || ($.errorCode ="AccessDenied*") && ($.sourceIPAddress!="delivery.logs.amazonaws.com") && ($.eventName!="HeadBucket") }" Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name "unauthorized_api_calls_alarm" --metric-name "unauthorized_api_calls_metric" --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace "CISBenchmark" --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: This alert may be triggered by normal read-only console activities that attempt to opportunistically gather optional information but gracefully fail if they lack the necessary permissions. If an excessive number of alerts are generated, then an organization may wish to consider adding read access to the limited IAM user permissions solely to reduce the number of alerts. In some cases, doing this may allow users to actually view some areas of the system; any additional access granted should be reviewed for alignment with the original limited IAM user intent.
input_row.default_value: By default, no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist for unauthorized API calls.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_unauthorized_api_calls_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls/Boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` and `describe_trails()` to list CloudTrail trails.
   - Use `get_trail_status()` to verify if the CloudTrail is active and logging.
   - Use `get_event_selectors()` to check if management events are captured.
   - Use `boto3.client('logs')` and `describe_metric_filters()` to retrieve metric filters from the identified log group.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` to fetch alarms associated with the metric filter for unauthorized API calls.
   - Use `boto3.client('sns')` and `list_subscriptions_by_topic()` to check for subscriptions to the SNS topic linked with alarms.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:** 
   - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
   - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
   - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
   - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?MetricName == <unauthorized-api-calls-metric>]"` 
   - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Multi-region CloudTrail trails
   - Corresponding CloudWatch Log Groups for the trails
   - Metric Filters that detect unauthorized API calls
   - Alarms associated with those metric filters
   - SNS Topic and its subscriptions

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Verify that the multi-region CloudTrail is active and logging.
   - Ensure the trail includes management events.
   - Check that metric filters contain the correct filter pattern for unauthorized API calls.
   - Ensure at least one CloudWatch alarm is active for the metric.
   - Confirm that there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS Topic.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass**: The CloudTrail is active and logging, captures management events, has the metric filter for unauthorized API calls correctly applied, has at least one CloudWatch alarm activated for this metric, and an SNS topic with active subscriptions.
   - **Fail**: Any of the above criteria are not met; for example, if the CloudTrail is not logging, if the filter pattern is incorrect, or if there are no active subscriptions to the SNS topic.

This automated approach ensures that the monitoring of unauthorized API calls through CloudTrail logs is effectively configured and compliant with the defined CIS controls, leveraging AWS APIs for verification.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. The assessment utilizes AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands, which are appropriate for automating the verification of CloudTrail configurations and associated monitoring resources.
- The use of AWS SDK capabilities and CLI commands is valid for checking the configuration of CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS, which aligns with AWS best practices for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are mostly accurate and valid for the intended checks. However, there are minor improvements that could be made for clarity and completeness.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_unauthorized_api_calls_configured` is descriptive but could be more specific to include details about the SNS subscription check.
- The automation details are technically correct, but the process could be enhanced by specifying the exact filter pattern for unauthorized API calls, such as `"$.errorCode = \"*UnauthorizedOperation\" || $.errorCode = \"AccessDenied*\""`.
- The AWS CLI command for describing alarms could be improved by ensuring it checks for the specific state of the alarms (e.g., `StateValue`).

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Filter Pattern Specification:** Explicitly mention the filter pattern for unauthorized API calls to ensure clarity and accuracy.
- **Alarm State Check:** Enhance the alarm verification by checking the `StateValue` to ensure the alarm is active.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudtrail_unauthorized_api_calls_monitoring_configured` to better reflect the comprehensive nature of the checks, including SNS subscriptions.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules to automate continuous compliance checks for CloudTrail and CloudWatch configurations.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and utilizes appropriate AWS services and methods for automation. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the suggested improvements are minor.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modify** the assessment to include the specific filter pattern for unauthorized API calls and ensure the alarm state is checked.
- **Consider renaming** the program to better reflect the scope of the checks, including SNS subscription verification.
- The overall approach remains automated, but these modifications will enhance the clarity and effectiveness of the audit process.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach can be automated using AWS APIs and CLI commands. The necessary checks for CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS configurations can be performed using boto3 and AWS CLI, which are capable of verifying the presence of metric filters, alarms, and SNS subscriptions. The technical accuracy of the proposed automation steps is high, and the required AWS services and properties are accessible via the AWS SDK.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_cloudtrail_unauthorized_api_calls_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()`
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - CloudTrail is multi-region and active
  - Metric filters for unauthorized API calls exist in CloudWatch Logs
  - Alarms are configured for these metric filters
  - SNS topic has active subscriptions
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - CloudTrail is multi-region and logging
  - Metric filters detect unauthorized API calls using the pattern `"$.errorCode = \"*UnauthorizedOperation\" || $.errorCode = \"AccessDenied*\""`
  - Alarms are in place and linked to the metric filters
  - SNS topic has at least one active subscription
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - Missing any of the above configurations or inactive states

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested minor improvements, which have been incorporated into the final decision.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v6.0.0
